text:
The first scene feels sterile and clinical, washed in the reflection of glass and bathed in artificially cool light. I am aware of the emptiness, the orderliness, and the subtle hum of electric presence. There is a strange comfort in the tidiness, yet it is also slightly alienating.

In the second scene, vibrant colors and oversized letters break the monotony. The wall’s graphics shout ideas while screens pulse with moving images and text that wonders about presence and anticipation. Here, curiosity wakes within me. I am drawn to the invitation to think, to reflect among others, and the uncertainty transforms into a spark of expectancy. My mind begins to wander between the order outside and the possibility within this space.
